mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are not significantly positive during rate cuts (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals imminent corporate earnings declines, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad collapse in consumer demand, which forces equity valuations lower over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (t-stat < 1.96) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The positive predictive power of INDPRO vanishes during post-shock recovery periods, yielding a negative or zero Rank IC over consecutive 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.5 percent while the federal funds rate remains below 2.5 percent triggers inflation fears and policy uncertainty, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of observations meeting the trigger condition, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 0.5 percent during a late-cycle contraction signals weakening economic momentum, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are not significantly negative when GDPC1 growth drops below 0.5 percent in late-cycle environments (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling payroll employment indicates productivity shocks that boost corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields a negative or insignificant Rank IC over 5-day horizons, or profit margin data does not confirm the predicted mechanism.
